intro: Ctenopteris gradata, also known as the 'Australian Passion Flower', is a climbing plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris gradata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
